findings.problem: The artifact set still leaves bounded frame-limit behavior unassigned even though the design names it as part of the current immediate-exit problem. The workspace spec requires bounded frame limits to remain harness policy rather than product motion semantics, but no task tells the implementer how frame limits must request a controlled stop or where that behavior lives.
findings.evidence: `openspec/changes/implement-phase-b-motion-lifecycle/design.md:282` identifies `signal/frame-limit` as the current immediate-exit path; `openspec/changes/implement-phase-b-motion-lifecycle/specs/true-ls2k0300-port-workspace/spec.md:15-17` requires bounded frame limits to be documented as test-harness behavior; `openspec/changes/implement-phase-b-motion-lifecycle/tasks.md:44-45` only assign signals/operator stop handling and auto-start/auto-stop/auto-reset helpers, with no frame-limit deliverable.
findings.recommendation: Add explicit design/task language that assigns frame-limit behavior to the harness boundary and requires it to request a controlled stop rather than remain an implicit product-runtime exit path.

findings.problem: The modified smoke-wrapper contract is not fully carried into the design/tasks. The workspace spec requires `run_remote_smoke.sh` to preserve the remote runtime process outcome as the local smoke verdict while still retrieving logs, but the current artifact set only binds automation separation and a timeout-failure case.
findings.evidence: `openspec/changes/implement-phase-b-motion-lifecycle/specs/true-ls2k0300-port-workspace/spec.md:30-41` requires the smoke helper to return failure locally when the remote runtime exits non-zero while still collecting logs; `openspec/changes/implement-phase-b-motion-lifecycle/design.md:317-320,333-337` only mention wrapper failure on bounded stop timeout and separating automation inputs from runtime markers; `openspec/changes/implement-phase-b-motion-lifecycle/tasks.md:45-47` assign env pass-through and checkpoint review but do not require propagation of the remote runtime exit status.
findings.recommendation: Amend the design/tasks so `new/user/run_remote_smoke.sh` explicitly preserves the remote runtime exit code as the smoke verdict after log retrieval, and include that behavior in the checkpoint review scope.

findings.problem: The B-1 through B-5 verification-note deliverable still does not normatively require the expected markers for each note, even though the workspace spec makes that part of the accepted verification bundle. An implementer could satisfy the current task wording with commands and artifact limits but omit the marker expectations the spec requires.
findings.evidence: `openspec/changes/implement-phase-b-motion-lifecycle/specs/true-ls2k0300-port-workspace/spec.md:19-26` requires each Phase B note to document the exact command or env entrypoint, the expected markers, and what the artifact proves versus does not prove; `openspec/changes/implement-phase-b-motion-lifecycle/tasks.md:51-52` requires command examples, evidence paths, and evidentiary limits but does not require expected markers; `openspec/changes/implement-phase-b-motion-lifecycle/design.md:612` says Phase B docs must update expected evidence markers, showing intent that the task list does not currently bind.
findings.recommendation: Update task 4.2, and any supporting design text if needed, so each B-1 through B-5 verification note must enumerate its expected motion/control markers alongside the command/env contract and evidentiary limits.
